_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Scope analysis reports to various audiences that accounts for data sharing classification restrictions
reference_document_element_description: Ability to navigate the tension between data sharing for organizational value creation and data protection requirements, finding practical approaches that satisfy both objectives.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Ability SP-A-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-K-006 (RC, cosine: 0.472), SP-S-007 (SP, cosine: 0.470).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.1/10 (raw 0.306). Shared vocabulary: [data, sharing]. NICE-only terms [accounts, analysis, audiences, classification, reports, restrictions] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, approaches, creation, finding, navigate, objectives] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.849 (P=0.843, R=0.855). Balanced P/R — the 13-word NICE element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Scope analysis reports to various audiences that accounts for data sharing class" and "Ability to navigate the tension between data sharing for organizational value cr" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.504 > STS 0.306 (gap +0.199). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, sharing] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [data, sharing]. NICE-only: [accounts, analysis, audiences, classification, reports, restrictions]. WIDAI-only: [ability, approaches, creation, finding, navigate, objectives].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.199 with low STS 0.306. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
